name: Hadamard's Maximal Determinant Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The set of all n×n matrices with entries in {±1} and the supremum of their determinants, bounded by Hadamard's inequality det(H) ≤ n^(n/2).

VINIT.justification: Before any matrix is constructed, the domain exists as pure potential — no specific entries, no determinant value, void of constraint.

TANCH.justification: The terminal anchor that closes and contains the entire system — the upper bound that all Hadamard matrices must satisfy, the hermetic seal of the theorem.

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism: the directed transformation toward orthogonality — each row becomes perpendicular to every other row, advancing toward the maximal determinant state.

AREV.justification: Reverse/descent: the contravariant operation that undoes non-orthogonality, descending from arbitrary matrices back toward the orthogonal constraint space.

CLINK.justification: Composition: chaining the construction of each row such that the next row is built orthogonal to all previous rows, composing the full matrix incrementally.

IMSCRIB.justification: Identity/self-reference: the matrix recognizes itself through its own transpose — the Hadamard matrix is its own canonical form, self-witnessing orthogonality.

FSPLIT.chosen_element: branch into two candidate row vectors
FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication: one row-construction decision splits into two orthogonal branches — either the next row is +1-aligned or −1-aligned with the constraint subspace.

FFUSE.chosen_element: reconstitute full orthogonal matrix from both branches
FFUSE.justification: Multiplication: the two branches (both row choices) fuse back into a single n×n matrix that satisfies H^T H = nI, recovering the original orthogonality constraint.

EVALT.justification: Affirmative/success: the matrix is Hadamard — all rows are mutually orthogonal, determinant is maximal, the theorem's condition is satisfied.

EVALF.justification: Negative/failure: the matrix is not Hadamard — rows are not fully orthogonal, determinant is suboptimal, the extremal condition is violated.

ENGAGR.chosen_element: existence of Hadamard matrices for certain n only
ENGAGR.justification: Paradice: Hadamard matrices exist for n=1,2, and n≡0 (mod 4), but the theorem holds universally for all n — both existence and non-existence are simultaneously held in the domain.

IFIX.chosen_element: canonical Hadamard matrix record (e.g., Sylvester construction)
IFIX.justification: Permanent append-only record: once a Hadamard matrix is constructed and verified, it becomes an immutable witness to the theorem — the Sylvester construction is the canonical fixed reference.

phase_2.split_input: partial n×k matrix with k rows already orthogonal
split_outputs: T-branch: next row aligned with positive constraint subspace
F-branch: next row aligned with negative constraint subspace
phase_2.fuse_result: full n×n matrix H where H^T H = nI, det(H) = ±n^(n/2)

phase_3.void_description: 00 — no matrix exists, no determinant computed, pure potential space before construction begins
phase_3.true_description: 01 — matrix is Hadamard, all rows orthogonal, det(H) = n^(n/2), extremal condition satisfied
phase_3.false_description: 10 — matrix is non-Hadamard, rows not fully orthogonal, det(H) < n^(n/2), suboptimal
phase_3.both_description: 11 — paradice: the theorem simultaneously asserts universality (holds for all n) and existence-restriction (only for n=1,2,4k), both truths held without resolution

steps.domain_action: uninitialized matrix space ∅ — no entries, no structure, void state before any construction
Hadamard's inequality det(H) ≤ n^(n/2) — the terminal boundary that contains all valid matrices
row orthogonalization morphism — directed transformation toward orthogonal rows
sequential row construction — compose each new row orthogonal to all previous rows
branch into two candidate row vectors — split the construction decision into T-arm (positive alignment) and F-arm (negative alignment)
T-arm evaluation — candidate row satisfies orthogonality constraint with all previous rows
forward morphism on T-arm — advance toward maximal determinant by accepting this row
F-arm evaluation — alternative candidate row also satisfies orthogonality constraint
reverse morphism on F-arm — descend through the alternative orthogonal direction
reconstitute full orthogonal matrix — fuse both branches back into single n×n matrix H where H^T H = nI
self-adjoint verification — matrix recognizes itself through H^T H = nI, identity self-reference
compose determinant evaluation — chain the orthogonality verification into determinant computation
determinant achieves n^(n/2) — success state, Hadamard matrix confirmed
canonical Hadamard matrix record — permanent witness, Sylvester construction fixed in memory
existence paradice — simultaneously hold that Hadamard matrices exist for n=1,2,4k AND that the theorem's inequality holds universally for all n
contravariant descent — from specific Hadamard instances back to the universal inequality bound
self-reference loop — the theorem recognizes itself in every Hadamard matrix constructed
compose back to boundary — chain all orthogonal matrices back to the Hadamard inequality constraint
terminal anchor — all valid matrices satisfy det(H) ≤ n^(n/2), system closed

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Sylvester's recursive construction algorithm — translates the orthogonality requirement into explicit ±1 matrix entries via Kronecker product H_2k = [[H_k, H_k], [H_k, -H_k]]
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: row-orthogonality verification protocol — each new row communicates its inner products with all previous rows; if all are zero, row is accepted; otherwise rejected
phase_5.memory_mechanism: matrix storage as orthogonal basis — rows stored as vectors in R^n; orthogonality cached as Gram matrix G = H^T H = nI; determinant stored as ±n^(n/2)
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: greedy row-by-row construction — process rows sequentially from 1 to n; at each step, branch into two orthogonal candidates and fuse back to single matrix state
phase_5.alfs_store: Hadamard matrix catalog — canonical reference store of all known Hadamard matrices for n ≤ 1000, indexed by Sylvester construction order and equivalence class

phase_6.cycle_cost: per row: O(n) inner product computations; per matrix: O(n²) orthogonality verifications; per determinant: O(n³) arithmetic operations
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: arbitrary n×n matrix with ±1 entries, rows potentially non-orthogonal, determinant unknown
phase_6.post_cycle_state: Hadamard matrix (if exists) with H^T H = nI, det(H) = ±n^(n/2), or certified non-Hadamard with det(H) < n^(n/2)
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the cycle is entropy-conservative because orthogonalization is a reversible linear transformation (orthogonal matrices form a group); information is preserved through the FSPLIT/FFUSE pair, and the determinant is a pure function of the matrix entries — no information is lost or created, only reorganized toward the extremal state.

  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑡 𐑱)  -- [2] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (row orthogonalization morphism — directed transformation toward orthogonal rows)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑾 𐑚)  -- [3] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (sequential row construction — compose each new row orthogonal to all previous...)

        (.arrow 𐑗 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [8] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (reverse morphism on F-arm — descend through the alternative orthogonal direction))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [9]: μ closes the Frobenius pair

  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑙 𐑱)  -- [10] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (self-adjoint verification — matrix recognizes itself through H^T H = nI, iden...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑠 ⊙)  -- [11] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose determinant evaluation — chain the orthogonality verification into de...)
  (.arrow ⊙ 𐑱 𐑭)  -- [12] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (determinant achieves n^(n/2) — success state, Hadamard matrix confirmed)
  (.arrow 𐑭 ⊙ 𐑳)  -- [13] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (canonical Hadamard matrix record — permanent witness, Sylvester construction ...)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑭 𐑗)  -- [14] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (existence paradice — simultaneously hold that Hadamard matrices exist for n=1...)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑳 𐑠)  -- [15] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (contravariant descent — from specific Hadamard instances back to the universa...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑗 𐑱)  -- [16] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (self-reference loop — the theorem recognizes itself in every Hadamard matrix ...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑠 𐑡)  -- [17] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (compose back to boundary — chain all orthogonal matrices back to the Hadamard...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑱 𐑼)  -- [18] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (terminal anchor — all valid matrices satisfy det(H) ≤ n^(n/2), system closed)

  (hadamards_maximal_determinant_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT
